message:
test(create): convert colon-in-title dep test from println debug to structured assertions

Same assertion-hardening theme as the adjacent list-filter cleanup:
`test_create_deps_colon_title` had been left in a mid-debug state — it
called `br create` and `br search` and `br list`, each without
inspecting the exit status of the latter two, then dumped their output
via `println!` so a human could eyeball the result. The test passed
even when the markdown import silently dropped the dependency on a
title that contained a colon.

Rewrite to actually verify the behaviour the test was supposed to
cover:

- Run `br create "Task: With colon" --json`, capture stdout via
  `Command::output()`, assert exit code success, and parse the
  returned issue to pull out the blocker's ID.
- Run `br create -f <markdown> --json` (markdown file references the
  colon-bearing title as a dependency). Assert exit code success,
  parse the JSON response, and:
    * Assert exactly one imported issue.
    * Assert its title is "My Task".
    * Assert its `dependencies` array carries the parsed blocker ID
      from the first step — proving the colon in the target title
      didn't break dependency resolution on import.

Imports `serde_json::Value` at the top of the file; both subprocess
invocations now use `cmd.output()` instead of `assert().success()`
so we can read stdout. No production code changed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
